notes: This dataset covers 119 of ~622 source-list restaurants: 15 from an initial curated test batch (mixed boroughs/tiers), plus 104 from the official nyctourism.com/restaurant-week filtered listing for Chelsea, Chinatown, East Village, Koreatown, Noho, Soho, West Village, and Union Square (all Manhattan).
Populate the rest of this file from nyctourism.com/rw / OpenTable source data to reach full ~622-restaurant coverage.
Keep unplaced and needs-review restaurants in this dataset; the map only renders status: placed records with numeric lat/lng.

Several Michelin star / Bib Gourmand tier assignments (Hupo, Crown Shy, Dining Room at Gramercy Tavern, Dhamaka) are corroborated via secondary sources because guide.michelin.com returned a 403 on direct fetch during research — flagged in each restaurant own notes for manual double-check against the live Michelin Guide.

NARO is tagged bib_gourmand_or_critics_pick without holding a Michelin star/Bib Gourmand itself — included on the strength of nyctourism.com own Award-Winning editorial roundup; its geocode_confidence is matched (building-level) rather than verified.

A dedicated tier-verification pass (2026-07-21) re-checked all 104 neighborhood-batch restaurants against Michelin Guide NY and reputable 'best of NYC Restaurant Week 2026' roundups, upgrading 9: Tuome (michelin_star); Hav & Mar, HanGawi, Ishq, Markette, Momofuku Noodle Bar East Village, Pera SoHo, Balade, and Noreetuh (bib_gourmand_or_critics_pick). Restaurants like Pylos, Wallsé, and Perry Street were specifically checked and confirmed to have lost or never held a CURRENT star/Bib Gourmand (e.g. Wallsé lost its star in 2022, Perry Street in 2011) — their 'standard' tier reflects verified absence of a current distinction, not unchecked default.

OpenStreetMap Nominatim rate-limited several research agents mid-session; a number of restaurants were geocoded via the US Census Bureau Geocoder (geocoding.geo.census.gov) as a fallback instead, noted per-restaurant in geocode_source.
menu_url / reservation_url values pulled from nyctourism.com listings were not independently re-verified; spot-check before relying on them.

restaurants.name: Gran Morsi
restaurants.notes: Official listing shows $60 Dinner and $60 Sunday Dinner offers only (no lunch offer). NYC RW rule: dinner = 3 courses. Direct booking link could not be captured from the JS-rendered CTA; check granmorsi.com or call (212) 577-2725.

restaurants.name: La Tête d'Or
restaurants.notes: Daniel Boulud's first steakhouse, opened 2025. Official listing shows only a $60 Lunch offer, no dinner offer and no Sunday participation. Too new for a confirmed Michelin/critics accolade at time of research, so tier left as standard rather than guessed.

restaurants.name: Empellón
restaurants.tier_source: NYT critic Pete Wells awarded Empellón Midtown three stars; also a James Beard Award finalist for Best New Restaurant (per official NYC Restaurant Week listing description, corroborated by independent web search).
restaurants.notes: Official listing shows only a $45 Dinner offer, no lunch offer, and no Sunday participation.

restaurants.name: Wayan
restaurants.notes: Official listing shows both a $30 Lunch/Sunday Brunch offer and a $60 Dinner offer, both available Sundays. Chef Cédric Vongerichten (son of Jean-Georges).

restaurants.name: Henry's End
restaurants.notes: 50+ year Brooklyn Heights neighborhood institution known for seasonal game menu. No citable star-rating source found, so tier left as standard rather than guessed. Official listing shows $45 Dinner + $45 Sunday Dinner.

restaurants.name: Hupo
restaurants.tier_source: Michelin Guide Bib Gourmand — held continuously since 2021.
restaurants.notes: Official listing shows $60 Dinner + $60 Sunday Dinner only. Bib Gourmand status corroborated via secondary sources (Hupo's own site, press coverage); Michelin's own restaurant page returned a 403 on direct fetch, so flag for manual double-check against guide.michelin.com.

restaurants.name: Le Pavillon
restaurants.tier_source: Michelin Guide NYC — one star.
restaurants.notes: Official listing shows only a $60 Lunch offer; course count not stated so standard NYC RW lunch convention (2 courses) applied. No Sunday price line shown, so sunday_available is false rather than assumed. Michelin star status corroborated by guide.michelin.com restaurant page (One Star) and press coverage of Le Pavillon during Restaurant Week.

restaurants.name: Crown Shy
restaurants.tier_source: Michelin Guide NYC — one star.
restaurants.notes: Official listing confirms $45 lunch and $60 dinner, both three courses; Sunday dinner ($60) listed but no Sunday lunch price shown, so sunday_available is true only on the dinner offer. Michelin one-star status confirmed via guide.michelin.com; a direct fetch of that page returned a 403 in the research session, so status was corroborated via search-engine excerpts plus secondary press coverage — flag for manual double-check.

restaurants.name: Francie
restaurants.tier_source: Michelin Guide NYC — one star.
restaurants.notes: Official listing shows a $60 Dinner offer only (no lunch), not Saturdays; course count not stated so standard NYC RW dinner convention (3 courses) applied. No Sunday price line shown, so sunday_available is false. Michelin one-star status confirmed via Wikipedia's List of Michelin-starred restaurants in New York City and multiple press descriptions of Francie as Michelin-starred.

restaurants.name: The Dining Room at Gramercy Tavern
restaurants.tier_source: Michelin Guide NYC — one star (The Dining Room at Gramercy Tavern).
restaurants.notes: Official listing shows a $60 lunch offer plus a $60 Sunday lunch/brunch; course count not stated so standard NYC RW lunch convention (2 courses) applied. Michelin one-star status corroborated via Time Out's roundup of Michelin-rated Restaurant Week spots; a direct fetch of the guide.michelin.com page returned a 403 in the research session — flag for manual double-check.

restaurants.name: Tanoreen
restaurants.tier_source: Michelin Guide NYC — Bib Gourmand.
restaurants.notes: Official listing confirms $30 lunch and $60 dinner, with a $60 Sunday dinner also listed (no Sunday lunch price shown); course counts not stated so standard NYC RW convention (2 lunch / 3 dinner) applied. Bib Gourmand status confirmed via guide.michelin.com restaurant page for Tanoreen and corroborated by Time Out's roundup of Michelin-rated Restaurant Week picks.

restaurants.name: Nami Nori
restaurants.tier_source: Michelin Guide NYC — Bib Gourmand.
restaurants.notes: Participation and offer ($30 lunch with corn rib appetizer + choice of temaki set) confirmed via nyctourism.com's own 'Top Tier NYC Restaurant Week Spots' editorial article, which also names Nami Nori a Michelin Bib Gourmand winner. A dedicated nyctourism.com/restaurant-week/ listing page for this Williamsburg location specifically could not be located (404), so exact weekday range and Sunday availability were not independently confirmed — sunday_available set to false pending confirmation. Bib Gourmand status further corroborated by guide.michelin.com restaurant page for Nami Nori.

restaurants.name: Coqodaq
restaurants.tier_source: Michelin Guide NYC — Bib Gourmand.
restaurants.notes: Official listing shows a $60 Lunch offer only (no dinner), running through Aug 31, 2026; course count not stated so standard NYC RW lunch convention (2 courses) applied. No Sunday price line shown, so sunday_available is false. Bib Gourmand status confirmed via guide.michelin.com restaurant page for Coqodaq and a Michelin-guide feature article; Coqodaq is the fried-chicken sibling of one-Michelin-star Cote Korean Steakhouse, reportedly sitting out Restaurant Week this cycle.

restaurants.name: Dhamaka
restaurants.address: Essex Market, 119 Delancey St., Store #34, Manhattan, NY 10002
restaurants.tier_source: Michelin Guide NYC — Bib Gourmand.
restaurants.notes: Official listing shows a $60 Dinner offer only (no lunch); course count not stated so standard NYC RW dinner convention (3 courses) applied. 'Sundays are optional' language appears on the listing but no Sunday price line shown, so sunday_available is false. Bib Gourmand status corroborated via James Beard Award coverage and press pieces identifying Dhamaka as a Michelin Bib Gourmand pick; a direct fetch of the guide.michelin.com restaurant page was not completed in the research session (search-engine excerpts used instead) — flag for manual double-check.

restaurants.name: NARO
restaurants.tier_source: NYC Tourism editorial pick — "Top Tier NYC Restaurant Week Spots" (Award-Winning Restaurants).
restaurants.notes: NARO does not itself hold a Michelin star or Bib Gourmand (its sibling restaurant Atomix, from the same chef-owners, holds two Michelin stars); included here as a critics'-pick on the strength of its own nyctourism.com listing (confirming $45 lunch / $60 dinner, Sun-Fri) plus its inclusion as an 'Award-Winning' highlight in nyctourism.com's own official editorial roundup of top NYC Restaurant Week 2026 picks. Geocoding matched the building address (610 Fifth Ave./Rockefeller Center) but resolved to a retail-tenant point rather than the restaurant itself, so confidence is 'matched' rather than 'verified'.

restaurants.name: Poppy
restaurants.notes: Weekday offer is dinner only ($45); the $30 offer is specifically a Sunday lunch/brunch (no weekday lunch listed), so it's flagged as a Sunday-only 'lunch' entry rather than a standard Mon-Fri lunch.

restaurants.name: Naks

Nominatim's top match for 201 First Ave resolved to a 'First Avenue Loop' feature in Stuy Town rather than a precise street-address point, so coordinates are approximate.

Only a $30 lunch offer was listed (no dinner); Sunday availability not confirmed, so sunday_available is set to false pending confirmation. Course count not explicitly stated; used convention.
